parsed.rationale: Region B contains a dense, thick bundle of fibers running vertically through the center of the brain structure. In contrast, Region A shows fibers that are more spread out and less concentrated. Region C displays a mix of fibers but lacks the singular, high-density core seen in Region B. Region D also shows a significant concentration of fibers, but the bundle in Region B appears visually thicker and more compact.
